title: Crunchy Indian Egg Whites Fritters
description: Light, golden fritters made from egg whites and gram flour, spiced with cumin and coriander for a crunchy, satisfying Indian snack that stays true to a sugarfree diet.
instructions: Whisk the egg whites until soft peaks form, then fold in the gram flour, yogurt, ginger‑garlic paste, cumin, coriander, chili powder, turmeric, and salt until a thick batter forms.
Gently fold in the chopped green chilies and coriander leaves.
Heat the oil in a deep pan to 180‑190 °C (use a thermometer or drop a small amount of batter; it should sizzle immediately).
Drop spoonfuls of batter into the hot oil, holding them close together to form round fritters.
Fry for 2–3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and crisp.
Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Serve hot with a squeeze of lemon or a low‑sugar chutney.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 days; re‑heat in a lightly oiled pan for crispness.
